query: What do I actually pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees?

queries: What do I actually pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees?

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text:
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

retrieved.title: Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text:
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

[## How much do I have to pay?](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/)

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and the Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Citizens of Non-EU/EEA countries pay the tuition fee from the first semester. The duration of studies is not relevant for the amount of the tuition fee. However, there are exemptions. Therefore, it may be the case that you pay the same tuition fee as EU/EEA citizens or only the Students' Union fee.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 726.72 Euro
- Total: 752.92 Euro per semester

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

If you only have to pay the Students' Union fee, you pay it with the programme fee for the Postgraduate Center. You do not have to take any further steps. If you also have to pay the tuition gee, you have to pay it within the payment period. Detailed information about the payment options is available in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: you have to pay the Student's Union fee every semester within the admission period, even if you are exempt from paying the tuition fee. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

If you pay at the university where only the Students' Union fee is prescribed, you have to separately pay the remaining difference at the other university. For example: You only pay the Students' Union fee at another university. At the University of Vienna you have already exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. A few days after paying at the other university you can verify in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee if the Students' Union fee has already arrived. retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
retrieved.text:
As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

retrieved.text: programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

Only then you can download your student documents. - What happens if I pay too late or too little? If you do not pay the full fee until the end of the payment period your degree programme will be automatically closed. You have realised that you have paid too little in due time (= within the payment period)? Transfer the remaining difference as soon as possible with the correct payment data. The required data (IBAN and BIC of the University of Vienna and your unique payment reference) is available in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,

reranked.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and the Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Citizens of Non-EU/EEA countries pay the tuition fee from the first semester. The duration of studies is not relevant for the amount of the tuition fee. However, there are exemptions. Therefore, it may be the case that you pay the same tuition fee as EU/EEA citizens or only the Students' Union fee.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 726.72 Euro
- Total: 752.92 Euro per semester

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text:
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

If you also have to pay the tuition gee, you have to pay it within the payment period. Detailed information about the payment options is available in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). reranked.text: u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period of the respective semester.

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: you have to pay the Student's Union fee every semester within the admission period, even if you are exempt from paying the tuition fee. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree programme at the University of Vienna. Please note: If a higher fee is prescribed at one university, it is best to pay there directly. If you pay at the university where only the Students' Union fee is prescribed, you have to separately pay the remaining difference at the other university. For example: You only pay the Students' Union fee at another university. At the University of Vienna you have already exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. A few days after paying at the other university you can verify in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee if the Students' Union fee has already arrived. Then you pay the prescribed tuition fee at the University of Vienna. You only have to pay the tuition fee, the Students' Union fee is forwarded by the other university. - I am studying a degree programme and a continuing education or training programme at the Postgraduate Center. reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities
reranked.text:
As long as you attend the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) you have to pay the course fee at the VWU first. Only then you can pay the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

- [Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,

selected.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
selected.text:
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

selected.title: Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
selected.text:
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

[## How much do I have to pay?](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/)

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

Citizens of Non-EU/EEA countries pay the tuition fee from the first semester. The duration of studies is not relevant for the amount of the tuition fee. However, there are exemptions. Therefore, it may be the case that you pay the same tuition fee as EU/EEA citizens or only the Students' Union fee.

[## How can I pay?](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/)

[## How do I apply for remission or reimbursement?](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/)

[## Am I eligible for equal status with EU/EEA citizens?](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/)

If you are currently attending the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU), you

- first pay the course fee at the VWU and afterwards
- the Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna.

[Detailed info about the payment](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/students-attending-the-vwu/)

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- [Decree regarding the tuition fee 2004](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010724)
- [§ 23 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/studienrecht/)
- [Students' Union Act 2014](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)
- [Decree on Groups of Persons 2018](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20010594)

- [Information on the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/)
- [Application and admission periods](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Administrative fee for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/administration-fee/)

[4] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee — Citizenship non-EU/EEA
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and the Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Citizens of Non-EU/EEA countries pay the tuition fee from the first semester. The duration of studies is not relevant for the amount of the tuition fee. However, there are exemptions. Therefore, it may be the case that you pay the same tuition fee as EU/EEA citizens or only the Students' Union fee.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 726.72 Euro
- Total: 752.92 Euro per semester

[5] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee — Non-degree programme
To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee each semester within the payment period. The amount of the tuition fee does not depend on your citizenship. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

Please note: a non-degree programme does not lead to a graduation with an academic degree.

You are in Austria with a residence permit? Please contact the MA 35 if examinations from a non-degree programme can be recognised for your residence permit. Supplementary examinations from the VWU are recognised.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

[6] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee — FAQ
or training programme at the Postgraduate Center. How much do I have to pay? If you only have to pay the Students' Union fee, you pay it with the programme fee for the Postgraduate Center. You do not have to take any further steps. If you also have to pay the tuition gee, you have to pay it within the payment period. Detailed information about the payment options is available in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already paid. - I am admitted to a continuing education programme at the Postgraduate Center and a bachelor/diploma or master programme. What do I have to keep in mind? If you are admitted at the Postgraduate and to a degree programme there are two options: 1. Only the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. No further payment or re-enrolment is necessary. You will be automatically re-enroled in your degree programme when the fee is received at the Postgraduate Center. 2. The tuition fee and the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you (due to your citizenship or because you have exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters) You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. In addition you have to pay the tuition fee (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union

[2] Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
There is a tuition fee and a Students’ Union fee at the University of Vienna. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and on whether you are enrolled in a degree programme or non-degree programme. The amount of the Students' Union fee is directly determined by the Austrian Students' Union. All students in Austria pay the Students' Union fee each semester to re-enrol in their degree programme and continue their studies.

To continue your studies, you pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. Citizens of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland are exempt from paying the tuition fee for a specified time period. This means: You only have to pay the Students' Union fee during the stipulated degree programme duration plus two tolerance semesters

Your fee is calculated automatically. You can see the amount of the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can check how many semesters of the tuition-free period you have left in u:space > Studies > Study overview.

- Students' Union fee: 26.20 Euro
- Tuition fee: 363.36 Euro
- Total: 389.56 Euro per semester

[3] Tuition fee/Students' Union fee
As long as you study, you must pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester within the payment period. In the first semester, you complete the admission procedure by paying the fee. In later semesters you thereby re-register for your degree programme.

Choose the applicable question below. You will be forwarded to a new page where you can find the detailed information you need.

Question: What do I actually pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees?

generate.raw_response: The amount you pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees depends on your citizenship and study program [2][4]. For EU/EEA citizens, the total fee is 389.56 Euro per semester [2], while for non-EU/EEA citizens, the total fee is 752.92 Euro per semester [4]. For non-degree programs, the total fee is 389.56 Euro per semester [5].

answer: The amount you pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees depends on your citizenship and study program [2][4]. For EU/EEA citizens, the total fee is 389.56 Euro per semester [2], while for non-EU/EEA citizens, the total fee is 752.92 Euro per semester [4]. For non-degree programs, the total fee is 389.56 Euro per semester [5].
